title: Microcos mildbraedii
taxonomy: Microcos mildbraedii is classified within the family Melastomataceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family. This family is part of the order Myrtales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The order Myrtales also includes other families such as the Myrtaceae and the Oliniaceae. Microcos mildbraedii is native to tropical Asia, and its classification helps to understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the region. The classification of Microcos mildbraedii also provides valuable information for botanists and researchers studying plant diversity and conservation.

The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. intro: Microcos mildbraedii, also known as the Asian trumpet tree, is a plant species in the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Microcos mildbraedii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Microcos mildbraedii is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the preparation of cough syrups and expectorants.
